text: The surgeon performs a shave technique on an endometriotic lesion involving the anterior wall of the rectum or sigmoid colon, using laparoscopic or robotic arms to lift the lesion. Sharp dissection is used to excise the lesion from the bowel wall, taking care to undermine the lesion while preserving muscularis tissue. The excised tissue is stretched over an EEA dilator. The defect is closed with interrupted sutures, first using 3-0 Vicryl for the muscularis and then 4-0 silk for the serosa, ensuring a two-layer closure due to the depth of the shave procedure.

The surgeon completes the repair of the bowel wall using overlapping fluoro silk sutures over micro sutures placed in the muscularis. The final repair is tested with rectal insufflation via a proctoscope. The procedure transitions from a shave technique to a full thickness disc excision, requiring resection into the mucosa to fully remove the lesion.

The surgeon completes the disc excision by inspecting the internal mucosa to identify and remove all diseased areas. The final defect is visible with the lesion removed. The closure begins with sutures at the apices, pulling the bowel to ensure a perpendicular closure to the bowel's length. The first layer closure includes through and through mucosa and most of the muscularis, followed by a second layer of interrupted silk sutures closing the muscularis and serosa. A staple disc excision is demonstrated, with sutures placed distal and proximal to the lesion, folding the unaffected bowel over it. A 29mm EEA stapler is positioned under the lesion, and stay sutures pull the lesion into the stapler jaws, which are then closed to excise the lesion and close the defect with staples.

The surgeon mobilizes the colon from surrounding structures, addressing a colonic lesion on the distal sigmoid colon that is densely adherent to the ovary. Rectovaginal fat is pulled over the staple line to reduce the risk of fistula formation following a hysterectomy.

The surgeon removes extensive endometriosis and completes a hysterectomy, revealing a full thickness bowel lesion in the anterior distal sigmoid colon. The mesentery is taken down, and a plane is developed underneath the colon using the intuitive vessel sealer advance. A distal staple line is created, followed by a proximal staple line location determination and collotomy creation. The anvil is brought through the vagina and placed through the collotomy, then pushed proximal to the staple line location. The proximal staple line is created, and the anvil is brought through it. The specimen is extracted through the vaginotomy, and the stapler spike is brought through the distal staple line to complete the anastomosis.
